Home / Blog / Online Astrology Reading
Astrology May 13, 2026 9 min read

Online Astrology Reading: How to Choose and What to Expect

An online astrology reading is now a normal way to access a serious astrologer wherever you are. This guide explains what actually happens during a session, how to recognise a competent astrologer from a generic one, what data to prepare, what prices are reasonable in Italy — and what you should not expect from astrology.

Editorial note — Article by the Holistic Unity editorial team. Updated on 13 May 2026. Informational content; astrology is a symbolic system for self-reflection, not a medical, psychological, or financial advisory tool. The prices and practices described reflect the Italian market at the time of writing. See the Sources section at the end for verifiable references.

What is an online astrology reading?

An online astrology reading is a video session with a professional astrologer who analyses your birth chart together with you. The session takes place on Zoom, Google Meet, or similar platforms; the astrologer shares their screen to show your chart and explains the symbols, positions of the planets in the houses, aspects, and current transits.

The format is essentially identical to an in-person reading — the only real difference is the medium. After 2020, online consultations have become the norm: they remove the geographic constraint (you can work with an astrologer in another city or another country), are easier to fit into a busy schedule, and often come with a recording you can review at your own pace.

In Italy, astrology falls within the unregulated professions framework: there is no state-issued licence, no national register. This means the real quality marker is the astrologer's training, transparency about their method, and their professional ethics — not titles.

The three most common types of reading

Online astrology readings come in different formats. Knowing which one you actually need helps you choose the right session and the right price level.

1. Natal chart reading

This is the most complete consultation and the natural starting point. The astrologer reads the chart calculated for your date, time, and place of birth: Sun, Moon, Ascendant, planets in the houses, major aspects. It serves as a map of basic tendencies — temperament, family environment, communication style, emotional patterns, vocational inclinations. Duration: 75–90 minutes.

2. Transit and forecast reading

A focused consultation that examines the planetary transits over the next 6–12 months. It makes sense once you've already had a natal chart reading — without that foundation, the transits would have no anchor. Useful at moments of choice: a possible career change, a relationship beginning, a move, a relevant family event. Duration: 60 minutes.

3. Synastry (couple chart)

A comparative reading between two natal charts: yours and your partner's, a co-founder, a parent. The astrologer overlays the two maps and analyses the recurring dynamics. The session can be done together or solo, but the most useful synastries are those that include both people. Duration: 75–90 minutes.

How to choose a serious astrologer

Since astrology is not legally regulated in Italy, the real quality filter is your own attention. Here are the criteria that, in practice, separate a competent professional from a generic one.

Green flags — signs of professionalism

  • Declared and verifiable training. The astrologer states clearly where they studied (CIDA, SOI Apotélesma, Kepler College, or other recognised schools), for how long, and which method they use (modern Western astrology, classical, evolutionary, Vedic).
  • Honest description of what astrology can do. A serious astrologer presents the work as a symbolic language for self-reflection, not as a deterministic prediction. They never replace medical, psychological, or financial advice.
  • Transparent prices, published on the website. No quotes 'on request' and no upsells during the session. You know what you're paying before booking.
  • Clear approach to the time of birth. The astrologer asks for your time of birth and, if you don't have it, explains how birth-time rectification works and what its limits are. They never pretend to know the rising sign without solid data.
  • Recorded session or written summary. Most serious online astrologers send an audio recording of the session or a brief written summary so you can review the key points without taking notes during the call.

Red flags — when to step away

  • Health, legal, or financial promises. “I can tell you whether you'll get well”, “you'll find love in three months”, “invest now in this asset”. An astrologer who pretends to make predictions of this kind is operating outside the ethics of the discipline — and, on health issues, can mislead you into delaying treatment that you actually need.
  • Pressure on additional packages during the session. Repeated rituals, “protective” talismans to buy, “essential” follow-up consultations at increasing prices. A serious astrologer reads the chart and ends the session.
  • Catastrophist or fear-based language. “Saturn will destroy your year”, “you have a terrible aspect”. The chart contains tensions, but a professional reading frames them as work areas, not as sentences.
  • No information about the astrologer. A site with only a stage name, no professional background, no transparent prices, no real reviews. If the astrologer is invisible, you have no way to assess what you're paying for.

What to prepare before the session

A bit of preparation makes the consultation much more useful. Here's what to do in the days leading up to the appointment.

Practical checklist

  • Recover your exact time of birth. You'll find it on the birth certificate available from your Comune (or, abroad, from the equivalent civil registry). An error of even 15–20 minutes can shift the rising sign and several houses.
  • Write down 2–3 areas you want to explore. Work, a relationship, an upcoming choice, a recurring pattern. The chart contains a lot of information: arriving with a focus avoids ending the call with the impression of having “heard everything and nothing”.
  • Have your data ready in writing. Day/month/year, hour and minute, city of birth. Send them to the astrologer at least 24–48 hours in advance, so the chart can be calculated and studied beforehand.
  • Choose a quiet space and good audio. Wired headphones, stable connection, no domestic background noise. The session is intimate work; an unstable connection breaks the rhythm.
  • Don't pre-study the chart for hours. A bit of curiosity is fine, but arriving with rigid interpretations gleaned from generic websites only filters what the astrologer is telling you. Leave space.

What you should not expect from an astrology reading

Honesty here is what separates a serious wellness service from a sales pitch. An astrology reading is not — and a serious astrologer will be the first to confirm — these things:

  • It is not a medical or psychological diagnosis. If you're going through depression, anxiety, persistent physical symptoms, or relational difficulties that disrupt daily life, the right step is a doctor, psychiatrist, or psychotherapist. An astrologer can be a useful companion alongside that path — never a substitute.
  • It is not a financial or legal forecast. No astrologer can tell you whether a specific investment will work or whether you'll win a legal dispute. Such questions require, respectively, a financial advisor and a lawyer.
  • It is not a deterministic prediction. The chart describes tendencies and timing windows, not events written in stone. Anyone presenting astrology this way is selling a product that doesn't match the consensus of the serious astrological community itself.
  • It is not a substitute for personal decisions. The reading offers a perspective, not an answer. The choice — to leave a job, end a relationship, move — remains yours, with all the responsibility that comes with it.

From a strictly scientific point of view, astrology has never demonstrated predictive validity in double-blind studies — the best-known reference is the Carlson study published in Nature in 1985, which found no statistically significant association between natal charts and personality profiles. This does not mean a reading is “useless”: many people find it helpful as a structured language to look at themselves. It simply means that's how astrology should be presented — and used.

How much does it cost and how long does it last?

In Italy, the prices of an online astrology reading vary based on the astrologer's experience and the type of consultation. Here are the realistic ranges as of 2026:

  • Natal chart reading (75–90 min): €80–€150 for experienced professionals. €150–€250 for astrologers with very long training (15+ years) and an established reputation.
  • Focused transit reading (60 min): €60–€100. Useful as a follow-up to a natal chart you've already had read.
  • Synastry / couple chart (75–90 min): €100–€200, depending on whether the session is done as a couple or alone.
  • Below €30 or completely free: with very rare exceptions (introductory sessions for new astrologers building their portfolio), prices like these signal either training-level practice or, more often, a content that is generic and not actually based on personal data.

One last practical note: payment usually goes through bank transfer, PayPal, Stripe, or Satispay. Always ask for an invoice — a serious professional issues it without hesitation.

Looking for a verified astrologer for an online consultation?

On Holistic Unity you find astrologers with declared training, transparent prices, and verified reviews. You book directly online and pay safely from the platform.

Find an Astrologer

Sources and references

  • Italian law on non-regulated professions: Law 14 January 2013, no. 4 (“Provisions on non-organised professions”) — the framework that includes astrology among the non-regulated activities — official text on Gazzetta Ufficiale.
  • Carlson, S. (1985). ‘A double-blind test of astrology.’ Nature, 318, 419–425. The most cited controlled study on the predictive validity of natal astrology; the astrologers involved could not associate personality profiles with natal charts above chance level. DOI: 10.1038/318419a0.
  • Italian astrological schools and associations: CIDA (Italian Centre of Astrology), the main Italian astrological association, active since 1971 — cida.net; SOI Apotélesma (School of Italian Astrology) for classical and modern training paths.
  • Free chart calculators (technical references): Astrodienst (astro.com) is the most widely used resource for natal chart calculation, with high-precision ephemerides — astro.com.
  • Online consumer rights in Italy: Italian Consumer Code (Legislative Decree 206/2005) — right of withdrawal, transparency on prices, and protection on remote services. Information available from the Italian Competition Authority (AGCM) at agcm.it.

Last reviewed: 13 May 2026. The Holistic Unity editorial team verifies links and regulatory references at each substantive update of the article.

Frequently asked

How long does an online astrology reading last?

An online astrology reading typically lasts between 60 and 90 minutes. The first session (full natal chart reading) usually takes longer, because the astrologer needs to walk you through the base chart. Subsequent sessions on specific transits or focused questions can last 45–60 minutes. Beware of very short consultations (15–20 minutes) presented as ‘complete readings’: there simply isn't enough time to properly explain a natal chart.

How much does an online astrology reading cost in Italy?

Prices vary a lot depending on the astrologer's experience and the type of reading. In Italy, an online natal chart reading typically costs between €60 and €150 for a 60–90 minute session. Astrologers with long training and an established client base may charge €150–€250. Short readings on transits or specific questions often start at €40–€60. Sessions offered for free or under €30 rarely provide professional preparation.

What data is needed for an online astrology reading?

To calculate the natal chart, three pieces of information are required: date of birth (day, month, year), the most precise possible time of birth, and place of birth (city). Time is the most important data point: without the time of birth, the Ascendant and astrological houses can't be calculated with precision. You can recover the exact time from the birth certificate available at your Comune. If the time is unknown, some astrologers practise ‘rectification’ — a technique that tries to estimate it from life events, but is not exact.

Is astrology a science? What does research say?

Astrology is not a science in the academic sense of the term. The most cited double-blind study on the topic, published by Shawn Carlson in the journal Nature in 1985, found no evidence that astrologers could associate personality traits with natal charts better than chance. An astrology reading should be experienced as a symbolic language for self-reflection, not as a clinical or medical prediction. A serious astrologer is the first to clarify this.

How does the astrology video session actually work?

The session typically takes place on Zoom, Google Meet, or similar platforms. The astrologer shares their screen to show your natal chart, explains the main components (Sun, Moon, Ascendant, planets in the houses), and then deepens the themes you're interested in. You often receive a PDF of the chart before or after the session. Some astrologers also send an audio recording, useful for revisiting key passages.

Can I do an online astrology reading from abroad?

Yes, this is one of the main advantages of online consultations. Working via video call removes geographic limits: you can have a session with an Italian astrologer while living in London, Berlin, São Paulo, or anywhere else. The only thing to watch is the time zone: agree with the astrologer on a time that works for both sides. Payment typically goes through bank transfer, PayPal, or Stripe, accepted everywhere.
